summary.intent: Fix WhatsApp inbound messages being parsed as empty (GitHub #534) by upgrading the whatsapp-rust ecosystem from 0.2 to 0.5, then add WhatsApp to the default offered channels list, and fix a QR code display race condition during onboarding.
summary.outcome: All three goals completed: 6 whatsapp crates bumped to 0.5 with full trait compliance (11 new methods per store), WhatsApp added to default channels across 4 locations, and a QR code polling fallback added to both onboarding and page-channels JS. PR #709 created, merged with main, review comments resolved.

repo: wacore and wacore-binary 0.5 default features include 'simd' which requires unstable core::simd::Select — must disable default-features on both in Cargo.toml
whatsapp-rust 0.5 requires a new 'tokio-runtime' feature flag and a .with_runtime(TokioRuntime) call in the bot builder (new 4th type parameter)

ProtocolStore gained 8 new methods in 0.5: TC token CRUD (get_tc_token, put_tc_token, delete_tc_token, get_all_tc_token_jids, delete_expired_tc_tokens) and sent-message cache (store_sent_message, take_sent_message, delete_expired_sent_messages)
SKDM methods changed signatures from String to Jid: get_skdm_recipients returns Vec<Jid>, add_skdm_recipients takes &[Jid]
channels_offered defaults exist in 4 places: crates/config/src/schema/runtime.rs (authoritative), crates/gateway/src/state.rs (fallback before config loads), onboarding-view.js, and page-channels.js
QR code for WhatsApp pairing is stored in plugin state via latest_qr() but was never exposed via REST or probe — only delivered via WebSocket event, causing race condition
just test runs with --all-features which pulls in CUDA/llama-cpp-sys-2 and fails on macOS; must run cargo test --workspace without --all-features to avoid CUDA-dependent crate failures
The 'beads' workflow is used for issue tracking — issues are created and closed via CLI commands around commits

code.finding: Added latest_sync_key_id: Arc<Mutex<Option<Vec<u8>>>> field to MemoryStore to track insertion order deterministically — DashMap iteration order is non-deterministic so get_latest_sync_key_id cannot rely on iterating the sync_keys map
Added two new sled trees: tc_tokens and sent_messages for the new ProtocolStore trait methods
Added two new DashMap fields: tc_tokens and sent_messages for the new ProtocolStore trait methods
.with_runtime(whatsapp_rust::TokioRuntime) must be inserted in the builder chain before .on_event() in 0.5
WhatsApp QR code is stored in plugin state via latest_qr() — added it to ChannelHealthSnapshot.extra so it can be polled via channels.status RPC
Two tests (channels_config_defaults_offered, channels_config_empty_toml) hardcode the expected default channel list and needed updating when whatsapp was added

workflow: After version bumps, use cargo check before cargo test to quickly surface all compilation errors before spending time on test runs
When resolving Cargo.lock merge conflicts, simplest approach is to take main's version then re-run cargo fetch to layer in new dependencies
PR review comments should be replied to via gh api with the pullRequestReviewComment reply endpoint and then threads resolved via gh api PATCH with resolved:true
Use time::OffsetDateTime::now_utc().unix_timestamp() instead of manual SystemTime epoch math for cleaner timestamp handling

friction: wacore/wacore-binary 0.5 simd feature requires unstable nightly API (core::simd::Select) that the pinned nightly didn't support — required discovering and disabling default-features on both crates
cargo test --all-features fails on macOS due to llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA dependency — just test recipe is broken for local macOS development
No REST endpoint existed for fetching the current WhatsApp QR code — purely WebSocket-driven, causing a race condition where QR events fired before the frontend finished subscribing
gh CLI doesn't have a direct 'resolve thread' command — required using raw gh api PATCH calls to resolve review threads
open_items: Manual QA needed: pair a WhatsApp device, send a text DM, verify it routes to LLM and produces a reply (not 'unhandled message type')
Users with existing WhatsApp pairings may need to re-pair (re-scan QR) if wacore struct serialization changed between 0.2 and 0.5 — should be documented in PR or release notes
just test recipe is broken on macOS with --all-features due to CUDA dependency — needs a platform-aware recipe or exclusion list

The QR polling fallback (channels.status polling every 2s) is a workaround for the WebSocket race condition — a cleaner fix would be to ensure the WebSocket subscription is established before triggering pairing, or to buffer the QR event server-side
